content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/kuadrant-console-plugin/source/src/components/GatewayPoliciesPage.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 43
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-page-banner]:
Component <Page> has a new prop `banner` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <Page> and determine if any should be moved to the `banner` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <Page>{content} other children</Page>
  After:  <Page banner=>other children</Page>

Incident context:
  componentName: Page
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: __ComponentReturn__

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Page
Prop: banner
To: React.ReactNode

Code context:
```
   42        </Helmet>
   43        <Page>
   44          <PageSection variant="light">

```

### Fix 2
Line: 44
Rule [sd-prop-value-pagesection-variant-light]:
The value "light" is no longer valid for the `variant` prop on <PageSection>.
Choose the appropriate replacement from the valid values: "default", "secondary"

Review the component documentation to determine which value best matches the behavior of "light".

Incident context:
  componentName: PageSection
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: light

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropValueChange
Component: PageSection
Prop: variant
From: light

Code context:
```
   43        <Page>
   44          <PageSection variant="light">
   45            <Title headingLevel="h2">{t('Kuadrant Policies')}</Title>

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/kuadrant-console-plugin/source/src/components/GatewayPoliciesPage.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1: `<Page>` has a new `banner` prop (line 43)
The rule says `<Page>` has a new `banner` prop that accepts `React.ReactNode`, and I should check if any children should be moved to it. Looking at the children of `<Page>`:
- There's a single `<PageSection>` child containing a title and content.

This is standard page content, not banner content. The `<PageSection>` with the title and data content is regular page body content, not a banner. There's nothing here that should be moved to the `banner` prop. **No change needed for Fix 1.**

### Fix 2: `variant="light"` on `<PageSection>` (line 44)
The value `"light"` is no longer valid. The valid replacements are `"default"` or `"secondary"`. In PatternFly v6, the `"light"` variant was replaced by `"default"` (the light background is now the default). So `variant="light"` should become `variant="default"`.
Let me verify the final state of the file:

The file looks correct.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-new-absorbing-prop-page-banner` | **No change needed** | The `<Page>` children consist of a single `<PageSection>` with standard page body content (title + data list). None of this is banner content, so nothing should be moved to the `banner` prop. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-prop-value-pagesection-variant-light` | **Applied** | Changed `variant="light"` to `variant="default"` on `<PageSection>` at line 44. In PF v6, the `"light"` variant was replaced by `"default"` (the light background is now the default page section style). |
